observe: Two of you, one shared thing between you. It only stays clean if you take turns.
fault: You didn't take turns. Now the value depends on whoever got there first — that's a race, and it's not one you want to win by accident.
repair: Watch it happen. See exactly where the overlap breaks it.
escape: Keep them from overlapping — you won't get a clean result otherwise.

observe: Concurrent threads sharing data can produce inconsistent results.
fault: A race condition has occurred on shared data.
repair: Observe the interleaving that causes the inconsistent result.
escape: Prevent the race condition before the cycle completes.

hints: The problem isn't the threads — it's what happens when their steps interleave.
